The earth is called upon, with all that are in it, to hear the prophet. God’s holy temple will not protect false professors. Neither people of high degree, like mountains, nor people of low degree, like valleys, can secure themselves or the land from the judgments of God. If sin is found in God's people he will not spare them; and their sins are most provoking to him, because they are most reproaching. When we feel the pain of sin, it behooves us to seek what the sin is that we feel pain for. People and places that are most exalted are most exposed to spiritual diseases. The vices of leaders and rulers will be surely and severely punished. The punishment matches the sin. What they gave to idols will never prosper or do them any good. What is gained by one lust is wasted on another.

The prophet laments that Israel’s case is desperate; but do not declare it in Gath. Do not gratify those who make merry with the sins or with the sorrows of God's Israel. Roll yourself in the dust, as mourners used to do; let every house in Jerusalem become a house of Aphrah, “a house of dust.” When God makes the house dust it behooves us to humble ourselves to the dust under his mighty hand. Many places should share this mourning. The names have meanings which pointed out the miseries coming upon them; thereby to awaken the people to a holy fear of Divine wrath. All refuges but Christ must be refuges of lies to those who trust in them; other heirs will succeed to every inheritance but that of heaven; and all glory will be turned into shame, except that honor which comes from God only. Sinners may now disregard their neighbors' sufferings, yet their turn to be punished will come.
